summary: Re-validation of FinDash Security & Sharing (Workspaces) backend after main agent fixed the 3 critical issues from iteration_9. Re-ran /app/backend/tests/test_workspaces_security.py against public preview URL using same direct-Mongo seed strategy (TEST_WS_SEC_* prefix). 19/19 pytest cases PASSED (was 16/19). All three previously failing scenarios are now green. Additional ad-hoc verification confirmed the X-Workspace-Id middleware also blocks /api/cards, /api/accounts, /api/goals (not only /api/transactions) and correctly allows 200 when caller omits the header or sends their own workspace id. /api/health regression 200. Seed cleanup verified.
passed_tests: FIX 1 - GET /api/auth/session with valid cookie => 200 {valid, user_id, user} (mirrors /api/auth/validate)
FIX 2 - POST /api/auth/revoke-all-sessions => 200 (session_version >= 1); subsequent GET /api/auth/me with same cookie => 401 (sessions deleted, cookie cleared)
FIX 3 - GET /api/transactions with X-Workspace-Id of another user => 403 'Acesso negado a este workspace'
FIX 3 extra - same cross-tenant 403 confirmed on /api/cards, /api/accounts, /api/goals (middleware applies globally)
Cross-tenant negative path - same endpoints return 200 when no X-Workspace-Id header or when own workspace_id is sent
REGRESSION - GET /api/workspaces/me auto-creates personal workspace (free, is_personal=true, owner member)
REGRESSION - POST invites on FREE => 402 upgrade; on PREMIUM => 200 pending invite with token + expires_at
REGRESSION - POST /invites/accept/{token} as 2nd user adds active member; invite=>accepted
REGRESSION - PUT /members/{id} role=editor recalculates permissions to editor preset
REGRESSION - PUT/DELETE on owner member => 400 'Owner nao pode ser alterado/removido'
critical_code_review_comments: iter9 review observations still apply (non-blocking): server.py is large and re-declares routes instead of using include_router (routes/auth.py + routes/workspaces.py still effectively orphaned), workspace_audit lacks index on (workspace_id, created_at), accept_invite does not verify accepting user's email matches inv.email (invite-by-link semantics).

Middleware workspace_header_guard (server.py:60-95) is the right place for cross-tenant enforcement; verified it short-circuits BEFORE the route handler so all current and future resource endpoints are protected by default - good. Recommend documenting the bypass list (health, auth/*, workspaces/me) and adding a unit test for an unknown workspace id (currently the test only covers another user's valid workspace).
test_credentials: No real OAuth credentials needed; tests seed TEST_WS_SEC_* users + session cookies directly in MongoDB and tear them down in module fixture.
seed_data_creation: Per-run: 2 main users + sessions, 1 throwaway user for revoke-all-sessions test, plus an extra ad-hoc pair (A/B) for middleware spot-check on cards/accounts/goals. All cleaned up (verified counts).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 critical fixes confirmed. No further backend retest required for this feature unless workspace/sharing logic is modified. Frontend testing of Workspaces UI is still pending (was deferred by this iteration's scope = backend only).
rca of the issue: N/A - all previously failing items resolved. Root causes from iteration_9 (manual route duplication in server.py + missing cross-tenant guard) were addressed by: (1) adding @app.get('/api/auth/session') alias mapped to auth_validate handler, (2) rewriting revoke_all_sessions to bump session_version + delete_many user_sessions + delete_cookie, (3) adding @app.middleware('http') workspace_header_guard that resolves X-Workspace-Id against workspace_members for the authenticated user.
